steps.title: Use vertex form
steps.content:
A parabola with vertex $(8,-18)$ can be written as
$$y=a(x-8)^2-18$$
Since the vertex is below the $x$-axis and the parabola intersects the $x$-axis twice, it must open upward, so $a>0$.

steps.title: Connect to the target sum
steps.content:
For $y=ax^2+bx+c$, the value $a+b+c$ equals the function value at $x=1$. Substitute $x=1$ into vertex form.
$$a+b+c=a(1-8)^2-18=49a-18$$

steps.title: Use the positive coefficient
steps.content: Because $a>0$, the value $49a-18$ must be greater than $-18$. The only listed value greater than $-18$ is $-16$, and it gives $$49a-18=-16$$ $$a=\frac{2}{49}$$ The answer is D.
